question: What are common failure modes in multi-agent systems?

claim_lines: Common failure modes in multi-agent systems are dominated by coordination, role/hand-off, and verification problems. A trace-based empirical study found 14 failure modes grouped into system design issues, inter-agent misalignment, and task verification, which matches that pattern. https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.13657

Direct evidence also shows that multi-agent setups can add communication and coordination overhead, and that naive handoffs can confuse the receiving agent or waste tokens if context is passed too broadly. https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s44336-024-00009-2 https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/multi-agent/handoffs

- coordination overhead — covered: surveys note significant communication/coordination overhead in large decentralized MAS, and official docs warn that context management and token costs matter. https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s44336-024-00009-2 https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/multi-agent/index

- role confusion — covered as an inference: the survey contrasts unordered cooperation without fixed role assignments with ordered cooperation using assigned roles and expertise. https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s44336-024-00009-2
- error propagation — covered: polling/aggregation can amplify shared misconceptions because model errors are correlated. https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.06612
- consensus without correctness — covered: more agreement does not reliably mean more truth in unverified domains. https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.06612
- evaluation difficulty — covered: failure attribution is hard because MAS traces are nondeterministic, natural-language, and partially observable. https://openreview.net/forum?id=kLLYJ6Bm7n
